meta_title: For Founders: passive income to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to passive income and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Passive Income to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no project ideas' trap. Learn to build passive income SaaS products using Antigravity and Claude Code in this beginner-friendly case study.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' paralysis stems from over-engineering. Solve this by building micro-SaaS tools that solve one specific problem for a niche audience. Leverage Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code to automate your development, focusing on recurring revenue models that require minimal maintenance after the initial launch.
content.intro:
The blank screen of an IDE is the graveyard of ambition. You have the drive to build, but you are paralyzed by the 'what.' This isn't just about coding; it's about the psychological friction of choosing a direction when the market feels saturated. Most founders fail because they look for the 'next big thing' instead of the next profitable micro-problem.

We are shifting the paradigm. By treating your development process as a factory for passive income, you stop chasing perfection and start chasing validation. Using Antigravity and Claude Code, we will strip away the complexity of the stack, allowing you to deploy high-value, low-maintenance utilities that solve real-world problems while you sleep.

steps.title: Validate the Micro-Problem
steps.description: Identify a repetitive manual task in a specific professional niche (e.g., real estate lead formatting). If people are complaining about it on Reddit, it's a candidate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS architect. Identify 3 micro-SaaS ideas that solve specific, high-friction administrative tasks for real estate agents. For each, output the core value proposition and the potential monthly subscription price.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Research top 10 pain points for real estate CRM users on Reddit and suggest a micro-SaaS solution for one.'

steps.title: Rapid Scaffolding with Antigravity
steps.description: Generate your project structure instantly to avoid the 'blank page' syndrome. Focus on the core logic, not the boilerplate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, production-ready Next.js project scaffold with Tailwind CSS, Supabase authentication, and Stripe checkout integration. Focus on a minimal architecture.

steps.title: Develop Core Logic with Claude Code
steps.description: Use AI to write the functional backend code. You define the business rules; the AI handles the syntax and edge cases.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure API route for a SaaS that processes CSV files for real estate leads and outputs a formatted JSON file. Include error handling for malformed data.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a secure serverless function in /app/api/process-leads that accepts CSV uploads and transforms data using standard regex patterns.'

steps.title: Implement Stripe for Passive Revenue
steps.description: Your product isn't a project unless it collects money. Integrate Stripe early to validate market demand.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a clean implementation guide for Stripe Checkout in a Next.js 14 app, focusing on the webhook handling for subscription status updates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Integrate stripe-node and create a webhook listener to update user subscription status in the Supabase database.'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Move from local environment to live URL. Speed-to-market is your competitive advantage.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a checklist for deploying a Next.js application to Vercel, including environment variable management and domain configuration.

pro_tips: Focus on 'boring' problems; high-glamour ideas are harder to monetize and easier for competitors to clone.
Always build the landing page before the app; if nobody clicks the 'Buy' button, don't write the code.
Use Supabase RLS (Row Level Security) to handle auth logic so you don't have to write complex backend middleware.
Limit your MVP to exactly 3 features; anything more is a liability that increases your maintenance overhead.
Automate your customer feedback loop by embedding a simple Tally form directly into the app's dashboard.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, spend 80% of your time on cold outreach to your target niche. Your goal is to get your first 10 paying users to validate the recurring revenue model.
content.conversion_block: Stop dreaming about the 'perfect' idea and start building the 'profitable' one. Your code is the asset, and your speed is the moat. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
